title: Customer Support Resolution Agent
officialDescription: You are building a customer support resolution agent using the Claude Agent SDK. The agent handles high-ambiguity requests like returns, billing disputes, and account issues. It has access to your backend systems through custom Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools (get_customer, lookup_order, process_refund, escalate_to_human). Your target is 80%+ first-contact resolution while knowing when to escalate.
description: Build a coordinator agent that handles customer support requests across refunds, billing disputes, and account issues. The agent must route tasks to the correct tools, enforce business rules (identity verification before financial operations), and escalate when policy is ambiguous or the customer requests it. Target: 80%+ first-contact resolution.
architecture_notes: Use programmatic prerequisites to block process_refund until get_customer has returned a verified customer ID — prompt instructions alone have non-zero failure rate for critical business logic. Implement escalation criteria with explicit few-shot examples in the system prompt. Use structured handoff summaries (customer ID, root cause, recommended action) when escalating. Return structured MCP error context including errorCategory, isRetryable, and what was attempted.
key_patterns: programmatic prerequisite gates (not just prompt instructions)
explicit escalation criteria with few-shot examples
structured handoff summaries for human escalation
MCP error taxonomy: transient / validation / permission / business
